phrasal_verbs.meaning: put something in a rubbish bin

A.title: Topic vocabulary (gap fill)
A.instructions: Complete the sentences with words from the box.

questions.sentence: The doors are ......, so they open when someone walks towards them.
I took my ...... to the library so I could finish my essay.
The team carried out years of ...... before testing the new medicine.
Do you know how to ...... this new 3D printer?
The physics teacher showed us some special ...... for the experiment.
Most students prefer ...... books because they can read them on a tablet.
The instructions were so ...... that nobody understood them.
Astronomers hope to ...... new planets outside our solar system.
This little kitchen ...... can cut vegetables in seconds.
The robot has a ...... design that makes it different from all the others.
The scientists worked late in the ...... to finish the test.
New ...... can help doctors find illnesses earlier.

B.title: Circle the correct word
B.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

questions.sentence: Can you **estimate / involve** how long the experiment will take?
There was a **sudden / minimum** flash, and the screen went black.
The toy is made of strong **hardware / plastic**.
We need the **exact / artificial** weight of the object.
The computer **screen / effect** is too bright.
Using less electricity will **decrease / invent** our monthly bills.
This program can **run / operate** on any modern laptop.
The machine doesn't work well because it **lacks / estimates** power.

F.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.

questions.sentence: The soup was ...... when I took it off the cooker. (BOIL)

We did a ...... experiment in class today. (CHEMIST)

The first moon landing was a ...... moment. (HISTORY)
The scientist checked the ...... three times. (MEASURE)
